title: Linear Search in array

content.problemStatement:
Write a program to search for a specific element in an array and print "**Yes**" if the element is present, otherwise print "**No**".


Input Format

The first line contains an integer nnn, the length of the array and kkk, the element to be search.
The second line contains nnn space-separated integers representing the elements of the array.


Output Format

Print "**Yes**" if the element kkk is present in the array.
Print "**No**" if the element kkk is not present in the array.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains two integers n and k — the length of the array and the element to search for. The second line contains n space‑separated integers a₁, a₂, …, aₙ.
content.outputFormat: For each test case print "Yes" if k is present in the array, otherwise print "No". Each answer is printed on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The element 1 appears in the array, so the answer is Yes.
The element 4 is not present in the array, therefore the output is No.
The element 5 appears as the last element, so the program prints Yes.

solution.approach: The task is to check the presence of a value k in an array. The simplest optimal method is a linear scan: iterate over the n elements and stop as soon as k is found. This runs in O(n) time per test case and uses O(1) extra memory. Because the total size over all test cases is at most 10⁶, a linear scan easily meets the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));String line;while((line=br.readLine())!=null&&line.trim().isEmpty());if(line==null)return;int T=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());StringBuilder out=new StringBuilder();for(int tc=0;tc<T;tc++){while((line=br.readLine())!=null&&line.trim().isEmpty());if(line==null)break;StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(line);int n=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int k=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int read=0;boolean found=false;while(read<n){line=br.readLine();if(line==null)break;if(line.trim().isEmpty())continue;StringTokenizer st2=new StringTokenizer(line);while(st2.hasMoreTokens()&&read<n){int val=Integer.parseInt(st2.nextToken());if(val==k)found=true;read++;}}out.append(found?"Yes":"No");if(tc<T-1)out.append('\n');}System.out.print(out.toString());}}
